# build-your-own-taskman

> A personal task manager published as a spec, not as code. It's a menu of
> features with a cross-feature dependency graph; select a subset, resolve
> prerequisites, and have an LLM build your own version. Two novel ideas: tasks
> are plain Markdown files (no DB), and a terminal runs inside the task app
> (which enables a live multi-agent supervision layer).

## Start here
- [README](README.md): what this is, the human-in-the-loop thesis, 4-step usage
- [MENU](MENU.md): the à-la-carte feature list (9 epics), tick what you want
- [DEPENDENCIES](DEPENDENCIES.md): the graph + machine-readable adjacency list
- [PROFILES](PROFILES.md): ready-made bundles (Minimal / Solo-agent / Cockpit)

## Prompts (the build pipeline)
- [00-resolve](prompts/00-resolve.md): close prerequisites, emit build order
- [01-build](prompts/01-build.md): the build runbook / constitution
- [02-verify](prompts/02-verify.md): acceptance-criteria self-check

## Features
One spec file per feature under features/. IDs are <Epic><NN> (e.g. A01, F01).
Each has: What, Why it exists, EARS acceptance criteria, Build notes.
Novel ideas: features/A01-task-as-markdown.md, features/F01-terminal-inside-app.md
